title: Dev Server Request Handling
description: Bun-based dev server routing: receive HTTP request, match route, check generated page cache or static file, respond or 404.
startup_sequence: Import static HTML pages (homepage, cheatsheet, gallery, privacy)
Call serve({ port, routes, fetch }) to start server on PORT || 3000
request_handling_flow: MATCH route against registered route table (Bun router)
→ Return pre-imported HTML module directly (no file I/O)
→ Sanitize param (replace /[^a-z0-9-]/gi with '') to prevent path traversal
YES → Response(file, {Content-Type: text/html, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY})
→ Parse URL pathname; pathname.includes('..')? → Response('Bad Request', {status: 400})
→ /js/* special case: check public/js/ first, then fallback to './src/detect-antipatterns-browser.js' for specific path
key_functions: serveGenerated(pagePath): async → checks bun.file.exists(), returns 200 HTML or 404
generateSubPages(ROOT_DIR): async → pre-builds all sub-page HTML files at startup
getSkills(), getCommands(), getPatterns(): async API handlers from ./lib/api-handlers.js
getCommandSource(id): async → returns content string or {error, status}
handleFileDownload(type, provider, id): async → delegates to api-handlers.js
decision_points: Route match: static HTML page | generated sub-page | static asset wildcard | API route | fallback fetch
Path traversal check: pathname.includes('..') → 400 Bad Request
/js/* fallback: public path missing → try src/detect-antipatterns-browser.js
API command-source: result.error present → error JSON with result.status | !result → 404
security_controls: Path traversal guard on all wildcard and fallback handlers (.includes('..'))
Param sanitization regex on :id and :slug route params
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on all file responses
X-Frame-Options: DENY on HTML/asset responses; SAMEORIGIN on antipattern-examples
response_types: 200 OK + file stream + content-type headers (assets/generated pages)
404 Not Found (file missing or command not found)
dependencies: bun: serve(), file() — HTTP server and file I/O
./lib/api-handlers.js → getSkills, getCommands, getCommandSource, getPatterns, handleFileDownload, handleBundleDownload
